Mini project 2

Aubrey Graham

2025-03-24

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.2     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(tmap)
library(sf)
## Linking to GEOS 3.11.0, GDAL 3.5.3, PROJ 9.1.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE
library(tidycensus)
# use your API key to access the data
# note - you only need to run this the first time
census_api_key("441a4387a8aa678eade9bbc5199627531970784e", install=TRUE, overwrite=TRUE)
## Your original .Renviron will be backed up and stored in your R HOME directory if needed.
## Your API key has been stored in your .Renviron and can be accessed by Sys.getenv("CENSUS_API_KEY"). 
## To use now, restart R or run `readRenviron("~/.Renviron")`
## [1] "441a4387a8aa678eade9bbc5199627531970784e"
readRenviron("~/.Renviron")
# download the 2020 variables in the ACS5 data
vars_acs5 <- load_variables(2020, "acs5" , cache=TRUE)
glimpse(vars_acs5)
## Rows: 27,850
## Columns: 4
## $ name      <chr> "B01001A_001", "B01001A_002", "B01001A_003", "B01001A_004", …
## $ label     <chr> "Estimate!!Total:", "Estimate!!Total:!!Male:", "Estimate!!To…
## $ concept   <chr> "SEX BY AGE (WHITE ALONE)", "SEX BY AGE (WHITE ALONE)", "SEX…
## $ geography <chr> "tract", "tract", "tract", "tract", "tract", "tract", "tract…
# download the data and spatial data for tracts in NC
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)
nc_tracts <- get_acs(
  geography = "tract",
  variables = c(pop = "B01001A_001",
                veterans = "B21001_002E",
                veterans.18.34 = "B21005_003E",
                veterans.34.54 = "B21005_014E",
                veteran.55.64 = "B21005_024E"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2020,
  output = "wide",
  geometry = TRUE)
## Getting data from the 2016-2020 5-year ACS
glimpse(nc_tracts)
## Rows: 2,672
## Columns: 13
## $ GEOID          <chr> "37037020600", "37105030102", "37013930200", "370010201…
## $ NAME           <chr> "Census Tract 206, Chatham County, North Carolina", "Ce…
## $ popE           <dbl> 3557, 4170, 4865, 2708, 2198, 1817, 1996, 620, 955, 362…
## $ popM           <dbl> 693, 503, 571, 406, 404, 432, 373, 175, 192, 466, 305, …
## $ veterans       <dbl> 242, 508, 495, 231, 87, 144, 192, 104, 59, 320, 170, 53…
## $ B21001_002M    <dbl> 112, 153, 216, 124, 78, 59, 118, 51, 41, 89, 62, 193, 6…
## $ veterans.18.34 <dbl> 24, 0, 0, 0, 17, 49, 11, 2, 0, 34, 16, 0, 0, 0, 29, 47,…
## $ B21005_003M    <dbl> 40, 19, 19, 13, 27, 43, 17, 5, 13, 35, 21, 13, 13, 13, …
## $ veterans.34.54 <dbl> 34, 202, 25, 28, 49, 27, 44, 12, 48, 25, 34, 84, 0, 32,…
## $ B21005_014M    <dbl> 43, 121, 39, 26, 52, 32, 52, 11, 39, 25, 26, 68, 13, 26…
## $ veteran.55.64  <dbl> 769, 694, 1485, 844, 308, 560, 469, 313, 263, 617, 681,…
## $ B21005_024M    <dbl> 228, 163, 341, 266, 159, 215, 184, 110, 119, 162, 139, …
## $ geometry       <MULTIPOLYGON [°]> MULTIPOLYGON (((-79.40216 3..., MULTIPOLYG…
map1 <- nc_tracts |>
  mutate("% of Total Population that are Veterans" = veterans / popE * 100) |>
#map our data
  tm_shape() +
  tm_polygons(fill="% of Total Population that are Veterans",
              lwd=0.5,
              fill.scale=tm_scale_intervals(style="jenks"))

map2 <- nc_tracts |>
  mutate("% of total Veterans 18 to 34 years old" = veterans.18.34 / veterans * 100) |>
#map our data
  tm_shape() +
  tm_polygons(fill="% of total Veterans 18 to 34 years old",
              lwd=0.5,
              fill.scale=tm_scale_intervals(style="jenks"))

map3 <- nc_tracts |>
  mutate("% of total Veterans 35 to 54 years old" = veterans.34.54 / veterans * 100) |>
#map our data
  tm_shape() +
  tm_polygons(fill="% of total Veterans 35 to 54 years old",
              lwd=0.5,
              fill.scale=tm_scale_intervals(style="jenks"))

map4 <- nc_tracts |>
  mutate("% of total Veterans 55 to 64 years old" = veteran.55.64 / veterans * 100) |>
#map our data
  tm_shape() +
  tm_polygons(fill="% of total Veterans 55 to 64 years old",
              lwd=0.5,
              fill.scale=tm_scale_intervals(style="jenks"))
tmap_arrange(map1, map2, map3, map4, ncol=2, nrow=2)
## [plot mode] fit legend/component: Some legend items or map compoments do not
## fit well, and are therefore rescaled.
## ℹ Set the tmap option `component.autoscale = FALSE` to disable rescaling.

## fit well, and are therefore rescaled.
## ℹ Set the tmap option `component.autoscale = FALSE` to disable rescaling.
## [plot mode] fit legend/component: Some legend items or map compoments do not
## fit well, and are therefore rescaled.
## ℹ Set the tmap option `component.autoscale = FALSE` to disable rescaling.
